files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,75 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "linkerd-10275-customize-mirrored-service-name",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "linkerd: Customize mirrored service name",
+    "description": "Customize mirrored service name. Requested by 6+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current linkerd deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your linkerd version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd\nhelm list -n linkerd 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working linkerd installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review linkerd configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant linkerd configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd\nkubectl get configmap -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=linkerd\n```\n### What problem are you trying to solve?\n\nI'd like to control how the local service name (in the source cluster) of a mirrored service is being generated."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Customize mirrored service name",
+        "description": "Hi , thanks for opening this.  It makes total sense to want to ability to customize the mirror service name so that clients can be agnostic to which cluster the exported service is running in.  I'm not sure that passing in a go template for the name is the right approach, however.\n\nOne advantage of the TrafficSplit based approach you mentioned above is that it gives you more flexibility and control over where the traffic is sent.  For example, if you have both `api-west` and `api-south` mirror\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd\nkubectl get events -n linkerd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Customize mirrored service name\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Hi , thanks for opening this.  It makes total sense to want to ability to customize the mirror service name so that clients can be agnostic to which cluster the exported service is running in.  I'm not sure that passing in a go template for the name is the right approach, however.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
